options.content:
Per-zone health snapshot inspired by intodns.com. Use the **Zone** selector at the top to switch zones.

**Top row** is boolean status checks — each row's Result renders `1` as **PASS** (green) and `0` as **FAIL** (red). **Middle row** is informational — the actual records / values returned by the queries, with no PASS/FAIL coloring. **Operator / debug views** at the bottom (collapsed) include `dnshealth_soa_serial` over time, probe cycle duration, query rate, cache hit ratio, and per-zone query duration.

**Demo zones in this stack:** `healthy.demo.` (all checks pass), `soa-serial-mismatch.demo.` (SOA serial divergence between primaries), `lame-nameserver.demo.` (auth NS doesn't actually serve the zone), `ns-mismatch.demo.` (parent advertises 1 NS but auth reports 2 different NSs), `missing-glue.demo.` (parent NS without glue, delegation walk fails entirely — won't appear in the selector since it produces no `dnshealth_query_success` series; the absence is the signal).

targets.legendFormat: Parent has NS records for the zone
Parent delegation walk succeeded

panels.title: Parent — status
panels.description:
### Parent has NS records for the zone

**Metric**: `dnshealth_ns_record{source="parent"}`  
**Why FAIL matters**: The zone may be unreachable — resolvers can't find the authoritative NSs without a delegation from the parent.  
**Investigate**: NS records (from parent) panel below; check the TLD's view of the zone.

### Parent delegation walk succeeded

**Metric**: `dnshealth_parent_delegation`  
**Why FAIL matters**: The delegation walk from root failed entirely — every downstream check has no data for this zone this cycle.  
**Investigate**: Exporter logs (`delegation walk failed`); root-server reachability.

targets.expr: (2 * vector(0)) + ((1 - vector(0)) * (clamp_max(max((count by (zone) (count by (zone, nameserver) (dnshealth_query_success{check="soa",zone="$zone"})) >= bool 2) or on() vector(0)), 1) or vector(0)) * (1 + (2 * (clamp_max(max(count by (zone) (count by (zone, nameserver) (dnshealth_query_success{check="soa",zone="$zone"})) == bool 2), 1) or vector(0)))))
targets.legendFormat: Multiple authoritative nameservers (>=2)
All NSs answered SOA authoritatively
No NS advertises recursion (RA=0)

targets.expr: (min by (zone) (count by (zone, nameserver) (group by (zone, nameserver, source) (dnshealth_ns_record{zone="$zone"}))) == bool 2) or on() vector(0)
targets.legendFormat: Parent and self report same NS records
All NS hostnames syntactically valid (LDH)
No NS hostname is a CNAME (RFC 2181 §10.3)

targets.expr: (max by (zone) (dnshealth_ns_classification_count{classification="self-only",zone="$zone"}) == bool 0) and on(zone) (max by (zone) (dnshealth_ns_classification_count{classification="parent-only",zone="$zone"}) == bool 0) or on() vector(0)
targets.legendFormat: No stealth NSes (parent and self agree on NS set)

targets.expr: (2 * (clamp_max(max(absent(dnshealth_ns_glue{source="parent",zone="$zone"})), 1) or vector(0))) + ((1 - (clamp_max(max(absent(dnshealth_ns_glue{source="parent",zone="$zone"})), 1) or vector(0))) * (clamp_max(max((max by (zone) ((count by (zone,nameserver) (group by (zone,nameserver,source) (dnshealth_ns_glue{zone="$zone"})) == bool 2) * (min by (zone,nameserver) (count by (zone,nameserver,ip) (group by (zone,nameserver,ip,source) (dnshealth_ns_glue{zone="$zone"}))) != bool 2)) == bool 0) or on() vector(0)), 1) or vector(0)) * (1 + (2 * vector(0))))
targets.legendFormat: Parent glue agrees with auth-reported addresses

panels.title: NS — status
panels.description:
### Multiple authoritative nameservers (>=2)

**Metric**: distinct (zone, nameserver) tuples in `dnshealth_query_success{check="soa"}`  
**Why FAIL matters**: A single NS is a single point of failure (RFC 2182). One outage = the whole zone goes dark.  
**WARN at exactly 2**: meets the bare minimum but RFC 2182 §5 recommends more nameservers for topological / provider diversity.  
**Investigate**: NS records panels below; add at least one more NS.

### All NSs answered SOA authoritatively

**Metric**: `min(dnshealth_query_success{check="soa"})`  
**Why FAIL matters**: At least one NS isn't responding correctly; further failures take the zone dark.  
**Investigate**: NS records (from the zone) — `Responded` column highlights which NS failed.

### No NS advertises recursion (RA=0)

**Metric**: `max(dnshealth_ns_recursion_available) == 0`  
**Why FAIL matters**: Open recursive resolvers can be abused for DNS amplification / reflection attacks.  
**Investigate**: NS records (from the zone) — `Recursion` column highlights the offending NS.

### Parent and self report same NS records

**Metric**: per-nameserver count of distinct sources in `dnshealth_ns_record`, aggregated `min by (zone) == 2`  
**Why FAIL matters**: At least one NS hostname appears on only one side — parent and zone disagree on the NS set, so resolvers picking either side may reach servers the other doesn't know about. The check is set-equality on **names**; IP-level glue disagreement is a separate concern (#37).  
**Investigate**: NS records (from parent) and NS records (from the zone) tables side-by-side — the hostname appearing in only one table is the discrepancy.

### All NS hostnames syntactically valid (LDH)

**Metric**: `min(dnshealth_ns_hostname_syntax_valid)`  
**Why FAIL matters**: Hostnames violating RFC 952 / 1123 (underscores, leading/trailing hyphens) may be rejected by strict resolvers.  
**Investigate**: NS records tables show each NS hostname — find the one with the invalid character.

### No NS hostname is a CNAME (RFC 2181 §10.3)

**Metric**: `max(dnshealth_ns_hostname_is_cname) == 0`  
**Why FAIL matters**: NS records pointing at a CNAME violate RFC 2181 §10.3; resolver handling is inconsistent → intermittent resolution failures.  
**Investigate**: NS records tables show each NS hostname; check its CNAME chain externally.

### No stealth NSes (parent and self agree on NS set)

**Metric**: `dnshealth_ns_classification_count{classification="self-only"|"parent-only"}`  
**Why FAIL matters**: At least one NS hostname is asymmetric — the parent advertises an NS the zone's own authoritative servers don't list (parent-only), or an auth lists an NS the parent doesn't (self-only, the "stealth" case). Self-only divergence can be a legitimate hidden-master setup (NOTIFY-driven primary not in the public NS set, by design) — verify intent before alerting.  
**Scope limitation**: This row detects asymmetry between sources we can query. RFC 8499 "stealth" servers — those absent from EVERY public source — are not detectable by any single-vantage-point exporter and remain invisible to this check. See spec 007.  
**Investigate**: NS records (from parent) and NS records (from the zone) tables side-by-side — the hostname appearing in only one table is the asymmetric NS. For self-only cases, check `dnshealth_ns_stealth_reachable{nameserver=X}` — 1 = working hidden master, 0 = leaked listing.

### Parent glue agrees with auth-reported addresses

**Metric**: per-(nameserver, ip) source coverage in `dnshealth_ns_glue`, evaluated only for NS hostnames present on **both** sides  
**Why FAIL matters**: For a nameserver the parent glues AND the auth answers for, the IP addresses disagree — the parent's glue points resolvers at one address while the zone's own A/AAAA records say another. Resolvers seeded from the parent reach the glue IP; resolvers that re-query the auth reach the other. Depending on caching and lookup path, end users hit different servers and see inconsistent answers. This is intoDNS's "Glue" check.  
**Scope**: Only hostnames present on both sides are compared, so a name missing from one side (out-of-bailiwick NS whose A the auth doesn't host, or a lame server) is left to the NS-set rows above, not double-counted here. The check is set-equality on the shared hostnames' (hostname, IP) tuples — an address present on only one side (e.g. a partially-glued anycast set) also FAILs.  
**N/A**: the parent provided no glue for this zone (every NS is out-of-bailiwick, or the delegation walk returned none) — there are no parent-side addresses to compare.  
**Investigate**: NS records (from parent) vs NS records (from the zone) tables — find the hostname whose `ip` differs between the two sources.

targets.expr: (2 * (clamp_max(max(absent(dnshealth_soa_serial{zone="$zone"})), 1) or vector(0))) + ((1 - (clamp_max(max(absent(dnshealth_soa_serial{zone="$zone"})), 1) or vector(0))) * (clamp_max(max(((max by (zone) (dnshealth_soa_serial{zone="$zone"}) - min by (zone) (dnshealth_soa_serial{zone="$zone"})) == bool 0) and on(zone) (count by (zone) (dnshealth_soa_serial{zone="$zone"}) > bool 0) or on() vector(0)), 1) or vector(0)) * (1 + (2 * vector(0))))
targets.legendFormat: All NSs report same SOA serial

targets.expr: (2 * (clamp_max(max(absent(dnshealth_soa_serial{zone="$zone"})), 1) or vector(0))) + ((1 - (clamp_max(max(absent(dnshealth_soa_serial{zone="$zone"})), 1) or vector(0))) * (clamp_max(max((count by (zone) (dnshealth_soa_serial{zone="$zone"}) >= bool 1) or on() vector(0)), 1) or vector(0)) * (1 + (2 * (clamp_max(max(min by (zone) (dnshealth_soa_mname_in_ns_set{zone="$zone"}) == bool 0), 1) or vector(0)))))
targets.legendFormat: SOA MNAME is in zone's NS RR set

targets.expr: (2 * (clamp_max(max(absent(dnshealth_soa_serial{zone="$zone"})), 1) or vector(0))) + ((1 - (clamp_max(max(absent(dnshealth_soa_serial{zone="$zone"})), 1) or vector(0))) * (clamp_max(max(min by (zone) (dnshealth_soa_mname_resolves{zone="$zone"}) or on() vector(0)), 1) or vector(0)) * (1 + (2 * vector(0))))
targets.legendFormat: SOA MNAME hostname resolves to A or AAAA

panels.title: SOA — status
panels.description:
### All NSs report same SOA serial

**Metric**: `max(dnshealth_soa_serial) - min(dnshealth_soa_serial) == 0`  
**Why FAIL matters**: Secondaries haven't pulled the latest zone — resolvers may serve stale data depending on which NS they hit.  
**N/A**: no NS answered the SOA query this cycle — there are no serials to compare.  
**Investigate**: SOA per-nameserver table below — find the NS reporting an out-of-date serial.

### SOA MNAME is in zone's NS RR set

**Metric**: `min(dnshealth_soa_mname_in_ns_set)`  
**WARN (not FAIL) matters**: The MNAME identifies the primary master. An MNAME outside the NS set is RFC-allowed and normal for hidden-master setups (NOTIFY-driven primary not in the public NS set) — so this is a soft 'verify intent' warning, not an error. If it's unintentional, NOTIFY may target the wrong server.  
**N/A**: no NS answered the SOA query this cycle.  
**Investigate**: SOA per-nameserver table — each row's `mname` label vs. the NS records tables.

### SOA MNAME hostname resolves to A or AAAA

**Metric**: `min(dnshealth_soa_mname_resolves)`  
**Why FAIL matters**: NOTIFY and dynamic updates target the MNAME hostname; if it doesn't resolve, both mechanisms break silently.  
**N/A**: no NS answered the SOA query this cycle — there is no MNAME to resolve.  
**Investigate**: SOA per-nameserver table shows the `mname` label — try resolving that hostname externally.

panels.title: NS records — from parent
panels.description: What the parent referral lists as the zone's NSs. Glue IP empty = parent did not include A glue (exporter resolved separately).

panels.title: NS records — from the zone
panels.description: What the auth servers themselves report as the zone's NSs (source="self" rows from the glue prober), joined with each NS's probe response (Responded = SOA query succeeded, Recursion = RA flag set). Empty Responded/Recursion cells mean the exporter has no probe data for that NS — happens when the auth's self-reported NS list does not match what the parent advertised, since SOA/recursion checks only run against parent-listed NSs.

panels.title: SOA — per-nameserver values
panels.description: One row per nameserver. Empty/missing rows mean the SOA query failed for that NS.

targets.expr: (2 * (clamp_max(max(absent(dnshealth_soa_serial{zone="$zone"})), 1) or vector(0))) + ((1 - (clamp_max(max(absent(dnshealth_soa_serial{zone="$zone"})), 1) or vector(0))) * (clamp_max(max(((dnshealth_mx_count{zone="$zone"} > bool 0) or on(zone) (dnshealth_mx_null_mx{zone="$zone"} == bool 1)) or on() vector(0)), 1) or vector(0)) * (1 + (2 * (clamp_max(max((dnshealth_mx_count{zone="$zone"} == bool 1) * on(zone) (dnshealth_mx_null_mx{zone="$zone"} == bool 0)), 1) or vector(0)))))
targets.legendFormat: Zone has MX records (or Null MX intentionally set)

targets.expr: (2 * (clamp_max(max(clamp_max((dnshealth_mx_count{zone="$zone"} == bool 0) + on(zone) (dnshealth_mx_null_mx{zone="$zone"} == bool 1), 1) or absent(dnshealth_soa_serial{zone="$zone"})), 1) or vector(0))) + ((1 - (clamp_max(max(clamp_max((dnshealth_mx_count{zone="$zone"} == bool 0) + on(zone) (dnshealth_mx_null_mx{zone="$zone"} == bool 1), 1) or absent(dnshealth_soa_serial{zone="$zone"})), 1) or vector(0))) * (clamp_max(max((dnshealth_mx_count{zone="$zone"} == bool dnshealth_mx_resolved_count{zone="$zone"}) or on() vector(0)), 1) or vector(0)) * (1 + (2 * vector(0))))
targets.legendFormat: All MX targets resolve

targets.expr: (2 * (clamp_max(max(clamp_max((dnshealth_mx_count{zone="$zone"} == bool 0) + on(zone) (dnshealth_mx_null_mx{zone="$zone"} == bool 1), 1) or absent(dnshealth_soa_serial{zone="$zone"})), 1) or vector(0))) + ((1 - (clamp_max(max(clamp_max((dnshealth_mx_count{zone="$zone"} == bool 0) + on(zone) (dnshealth_mx_null_mx{zone="$zone"} == bool 1), 1) or absent(dnshealth_soa_serial{zone="$zone"})), 1) or vector(0))) * (clamp_max(max((dnshealth_mx_cname_count{zone="$zone"} == bool 0) or on() vector(0)), 1) or vector(0)) * (1 + (2 * vector(0))))
targets.legendFormat: No MX target is a CNAME (RFC 2181 §10.3)

targets.expr: (2 * (clamp_max(max(clamp_max((dnshealth_mx_count{zone="$zone"} == bool 0) + on(zone) (dnshealth_mx_null_mx{zone="$zone"} == bool 1), 1) or absent(dnshealth_soa_serial{zone="$zone"})), 1) or vector(0))) + ((1 - (clamp_max(max(clamp_max((dnshealth_mx_count{zone="$zone"} == bool 0) + on(zone) (dnshealth_mx_null_mx{zone="$zone"} == bool 1), 1) or absent(dnshealth_soa_serial{zone="$zone"})), 1) or vector(0))) * (clamp_max(max((dnshealth_mx_count{zone="$zone"} == bool dnshealth_mx_syntax_valid_count{zone="$zone"}) or on() vector(0)), 1) or vector(0)) * (1 + (2 * vector(0))))
targets.legendFormat: All MX target hostnames syntactically valid (LDH)

targets.expr: (2 * (clamp_max(max(absent(dnshealth_soa_serial{zone="$zone"})), 1) or vector(0))) + ((1 - (clamp_max(max(absent(dnshealth_soa_serial{zone="$zone"})), 1) or vector(0))) * (clamp_max(max((1 - clamp_max((dnshealth_mx_has_null_mx_rr{zone="$zone"} == bool 1) * on(zone) (dnshealth_mx_count{zone="$zone"} > bool 1), 1)) or on() vector(0)), 1) or vector(0)) * (1 + (2 * vector(0))))
targets.legendFormat: No conflict between Null MX and real MX records

panels.title: MX — status
panels.description:
### Zone has MX records (or Null MX intentionally set)

**Metric**: `dnshealth_mx_count` + `dnshealth_mx_null_mx`  
**Why FAIL matters**: Zone publishes no MX records AND no Null MX declaration — all incoming email fails. Either publish MX records or declare Null MX (`0 .`) per RFC 7505 if no email is intended.  
**WARN at exactly one MX**: mail is deliverable but there is no backup MX — a single MX outage takes inbound mail down. Add a lower-priority backup MX for redundancy.  
**Investigate**: per-MX records table below shows what the zone advertises; check the auth's zone file for the MX RR set.

### All MX targets resolve

**Metric**: `dnshealth_mx_count == dnshealth_mx_resolved_count`  
**Why FAIL matters**: At least one MX target's hostname doesn't resolve to A or AAAA. Inbound mail attempts for that target will fail.  
**N/A**: the zone has no MX records, or is Null MX (`0 .`) — there are no targets to resolve, so the check does not apply.  
**Investigate**: per-MX records table — `resolves=0` column identifies the offending target.

### No MX target is a CNAME (RFC 2181 §10.3)

**Metric**: `dnshealth_mx_cname_count == 0`  
**Why FAIL matters**: At least one MX target is an alias (CNAME), violating RFC 2181 §10.3. Many MTAs handle this inconsistently; some refuse delivery outright.  
**N/A**: the zone has no MX records, or is Null MX — no targets to check.  
**Investigate**: per-MX records table — `is_cname=1` column identifies the offending target.

### All MX target hostnames syntactically valid (LDH)

**Metric**: `dnshealth_mx_count == dnshealth_mx_syntax_valid_count`  
**Why FAIL matters**: At least one MX target hostname has invalid syntax (underscore, leading/trailing hyphen, etc.) per RFC 952/1123. Some strict resolvers may reject.  
**N/A**: the zone has no MX records, or is Null MX — no hostnames to validate.  
**Investigate**: per-MX records table; the same LDH check is applied to NS hostnames in spec N6 — see that row for the validator details.

### No conflict between Null MX and real MX records

**Metric**: derived from `dnshealth_mx_has_null_mx_rr` + `dnshealth_mx_count`  
**Why FAIL matters**: Zone publishes a Null MX RR (`0 .`) AND additional MX records. RFC 7505 §3 requires Null MX to be the SOLE MX record; coexistence is undefined and likely interpreted differently by different MTAs.  
**Investigate**: auth's zone file — remove either the Null MX RR (if you want to accept email) or the real MX records (if you don't). The distinction between `dnshealth_mx_null_mx` (canonical form only) and `dnshealth_mx_has_null_mx_rr` (any Null MX RR present, regardless of count) is what makes this row catchable — see spec 008 audit D-5.

panels.title: MX records — per zone
panels.description: Per-MX details for the selected zone: target hostname, priority, resolution status, CNAME status, syntax validity, role (primary = lowest-priority MX; ties at minimum priority all read "primary"). Empty cells in resolves/is-CNAME columns indicate Null MX's "." sentinel target — those checks intentionally don't apply per RFC 7505. SMTP-level reachability is out of scope; use blackbox_exporter with an SMTP prober for that.

targets.expr: (2 * (clamp_max(max(absent(dnshealth_spf_present{zone="$zone"}) or absent(dnshealth_soa_serial{zone="$zone"})), 1) or vector(0))) + ((1 - (clamp_max(max(absent(dnshealth_spf_present{zone="$zone"}) or absent(dnshealth_soa_serial{zone="$zone"})), 1) or vector(0))) * (clamp_max(max(1 - clamp_max((max by (zone) (dnshealth_spf_record_count{zone="$zone"}) > bool 1) + (max by (zone) (dnshealth_spf_present{zone="$zone"}) == bool 1) * (max by (zone) (dnshealth_spf_valid{zone="$zone"}) == bool 0), 1)), 1) or vector(0)) * (1 + (2 * (clamp_max(max(max by (zone) (dnshealth_spf_present{zone="$zone"}) == bool 0), 1) or vector(0)))))
targets.legendFormat: Zone publishes a single valid SPF record

targets.expr: (2 * (clamp_max(max(absent(dnshealth_spf_terminal_all{zone="$zone"})), 1) or vector(0))) + ((1 - (clamp_max(max(absent(dnshealth_spf_terminal_all{zone="$zone"})), 1) or vector(0))) * (clamp_max(max(max by (zone) (dnshealth_spf_terminal_all{zone="$zone"})), 1) or vector(0)) * (1 + (2 * (clamp_max(max(max by (zone) (dnshealth_spf_terminal_all{zone="$zone",qualifier=~"neutral|none|pass"})), 1) or vector(0)))))
targets.legendFormat: SPF ends in a restrictive 'all' qualifier

targets.expr: (2 * (clamp_max(max(absent(dnshealth_spf_lookup_budget_exceeded{zone="$zone"})), 1) or vector(0))) + ((1 - (clamp_max(max(absent(dnshealth_spf_lookup_budget_exceeded{zone="$zone"})), 1) or vector(0))) * (clamp_max(max(max by (zone) (dnshealth_spf_lookup_budget_exceeded{zone="$zone"}) == bool 0), 1) or vector(0)) * (1 + (2 * vector(0))))
targets.legendFormat: SPF within the 10-lookup budget (RFC 7208 §4.6.4)

panels.title: SPF — status
panels.description:
### Zone publishes a single valid SPF record

**Metric**: `dnshealth_spf_present` / `dnshealth_spf_record_count` / `dnshealth_spf_valid`  
**WARN (absent)**: the zone publishes no SPF record. Even a domain that sends no mail should publish `v=spf1 -all` to stop spammers forging it as the sender — SPF protects the domain regardless of whether it receives mail (MX), so this applies to no-mail and Null-MX zones too. Verify intent.  
**FAIL (broken)**: more than one `v=spf1` record (RFC 7208 §3.2 permanent error) or a malformed record — receivers get a PermError and SPF effectively does not work.  
**Investigate**: the zone's apex TXT records.

### SPF ends in a restrictive 'all' qualifier

**Metric**: `dnshealth_spf_terminal_all{qualifier}`  
**PASS**: the record ends in `-all` (fail) or `~all` (softfail) — unauthorized senders are rejected or marked.  
**WARN**: `?all` (neutral), no terminal `all`, or `+all` — `+all` is syntactically valid but authorizes ANY sender to spoof the domain, which is reckless; the others assert nothing. Verify intent.  
**N/A**: the zone has no single SPF record (absent, or multiple records — see the row above).  
**Investigate**: the apex `v=spf1` record's final mechanism.

### SPF within the 10-lookup budget (RFC 7208 §4.6.4)

**Metric**: `dnshealth_spf_lookup_budget_exceeded` / `dnshealth_spf_lookup_count`  
**Why FAIL matters**: the SPF record requires more than 10 DNS lookups to evaluate (counting recursively through every `include:`/`redirect=`). Receiving mail servers return **PermError** per RFC 7208 §4.6.4 and SPF silently stops working — even though the record looks fine by eye. The offending lookups are usually buried inside third-party `include:` records.  
**Count semantics**: `dnshealth_spf_lookup_count` is exact for 0–10; an over-budget record reads **11 ("≥11")** — the walk stops the instant it knows it's over.  
**Trust**: a FAIL fires only on a *resolved* over-budget count. If a third-party include is briefly unreachable this cycle, the row stays PASS and `dnshealth_spf_lookup_eval_complete` reads 0 (the count is a lower bound) — no false alarm from a flaky include.  
**N/A**: the zone has no single valid SPF record (absent / multiple / malformed — see the rows above).  
**Investigate**: expand the apex `include:` tree; trim or flatten third-party includes.

panels.title: SPF record — per zone
panels.description: The actual SPF record the selected zone publishes, with the terminal 'all' qualifier and the RFC 7208 §4.6.4 recursive DNS-lookup count (11 means "≥11"). Raw record comes from the dnshealth_spf_record info gauge. A zone with more than one v=spf1 record (an RFC 7208 §3.2 PermError) shows every record joined by " | " in the SPF-record cell, with the qualifier and lookup columns blank (those are undefined for multiple records). An empty row means the zone publishes no SPF record at all.

targets.expr: (2 * (clamp_max(max(absent(dnshealth_dmarc_present{zone="$zone"}) or absent(dnshealth_soa_serial{zone="$zone"})), 1) or vector(0))) + ((1 - (clamp_max(max(absent(dnshealth_dmarc_present{zone="$zone"}) or absent(dnshealth_soa_serial{zone="$zone"})), 1) or vector(0))) * (clamp_max(max(1 - clamp_max((max by (zone) (dnshealth_dmarc_present{zone="$zone"}) == bool 1) * (max by (zone) (dnshealth_dmarc_valid{zone="$zone"}) == bool 0), 1)), 1) or vector(0)) * (1 + (2 * (clamp_max(max(max by (zone) (dnshealth_dmarc_present{zone="$zone"}) == bool 0), 1) or vector(0)))))
targets.legendFormat: Zone publishes a valid DMARC record

targets.expr: (2 * (clamp_max(max(absent(dnshealth_dmarc_policy{zone="$zone"})), 1) or vector(0))) + ((1 - (clamp_max(max(absent(dnshealth_dmarc_policy{zone="$zone"})), 1) or vector(0))) * (clamp_max(max(max by (zone) (dnshealth_dmarc_policy{zone="$zone"})), 1) or vector(0)) * (1 + (2 * (clamp_max(max(max by (zone) (dnshealth_dmarc_policy{zone="$zone",policy="none"})), 1) or vector(0)))))
targets.legendFormat: DMARC enforces a policy (quarantine or reject)

panels.title: DMARC — status
panels.description:
### Zone publishes a valid DMARC record

**Metric**: `dnshealth_dmarc_present` / `dnshealth_dmarc_valid`  
**WARN (absent)**: no DMARC record at `_dmarc.<zone>`. DMARC ties SPF/DKIM together with alignment and tells receivers what to do on failure; without it, look-alike spoofing SPF alone can't stop is unmitigated. A no-mail / Null-MX domain still benefits (publish `p=reject`). Verify intent.  
**FAIL (broken)**: a `v=DMARC1` record with no valid `p=` tag (RFC 7489 §6.3 requires it) — receivers can't apply a policy.  
**Investigate**: the `_dmarc.<zone>` TXT record.

### DMARC enforces a policy (quarantine or reject)

**Metric**: `dnshealth_dmarc_policy{policy}`  
**PASS**: `p=quarantine` or `p=reject` — the domain enforces alignment, so spoofed mail is quarantined or rejected.  
**WARN**: `p=none` — DMARC is published but only MONITORS; it does not enforce, so spoofed mail is still delivered. A legitimate rollout stage, but verify it's intended to stay there.  
**N/A**: the zone has no valid DMARC record (absent or malformed — see the row above).  
**Investigate**: the `p=` tag of the `_dmarc.<zone>` record.

panels.title: DMARC record — per zone
panels.description: The actual DMARC record the selected zone publishes at _dmarc.<zone>, with the parsed enforcement policy. Raw record comes from the dnshealth_dmarc_record info gauge. An empty row means the zone publishes no DMARC record.

targets.legendFormat: cycle duration

panels.title: Probe cycle duration
panels.description: Wall time of each probe cycle, from dnshealth_probe_cycle_duration_seconds.

targets.legendFormat: queries/s — {{server}}
cache hit ratio

panels.title: Query rate and delegation cache hit ratio
panels.description: Left axis: per-server query rate. Cache ratio = hits / (hits + misses).

targets.legendFormat: {{nameserver}}

panels.title: SOA serials per nameserver over time — ${zone}
panels.description: Each line is one nameserver for the selected zone. Lines overlap when primaries agree; divergence appears as parallel non-overlapping lines.

targets.legendFormat: {{check}} via {{nameserver}}

panels.title: Query duration (per check / nameserver) — ${zone}
panels.description: dnshealth_query_duration_seconds for the selected zone — useful for spotting slow nameservers or check types.
